entry.title: How Defense and Security Organizations can Benefit from SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The Rise of Artificial Intelligence and its Impact on the Defense and Security Industry

 

The global interest in Artificial Intelligence has risen dramatically since OpenAI first launched its AI-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, in November 2022. The technology nowadays is not only one that most companies and organizations are trying to implement into their operations, but also a technological turning point for most industries. This rapid evolution in technology is also reflected in $92bn worth of corporate investments into AI in 2022 and an extraordinarily high annual growth rate of 109% of the worldwide market for Artificial Intelligence in the period from 2023 ($207bn) to 2030 ($1,800bn).

The Defense and Security industry is a frontrunner when it comes to the use of AI. For one thing, defense spending, especially in AI, is significantly on the rise since a couple of years. The global defense expenditures, for example, rose to $2,240bn in 2022, whereas the spendings in Artificial Intelligence of the U.S. Department of Defense will be increased by 63.6% to $1.8bn in 2024. According to the “2024 aerospace and defense industry outlook report”, that was published by the consulting company Deloitte, the main focus of spending in technology within the defense sector will be on enhancing capabilities and improving readiness. This general trend is also reflected in rising investments into private defense companies. Anduril Industries, a US-based startup company in the defense sector, for instance, even received the worldwide second highest private AI investment of $1.48bn in 2022. Deloitte also emphasized the importance for military organizations of investing in digital transformation.

The advantages of applying Artificial Intelligence technology are not limited to defense organizations. Emergency services organizations can profit from AI in a similar manner. Good examples for this are AI-powered tools, that support emergency personnel during operations. Artificial Intelligence here can, for instance, optimize the medication of patients, or help to prevent and better manage natural catastrophes, like wildfires or floods. What also should not be underestimated in this context is the potential that AI brings for preventing potential emergency situations in advance. Nowadays technology is, for example, already capable of monitoring human health data in real-time, which can prevent people from experiencing diverse health issues, such as strokes or heart attacks. On the other side, Artificial Intelligence is also a core element of Internet of Things and Smart City concepts, where the technology is used for preventing different forms of accidents. Examples include sensor-based software solutions, that are able to evaluate potential risks at any time, and in case of necessity automatically initiate countermeasures, such as evasive maneuvers or automatic emergency braking. Such solutions are not limited to single cars but can be integrated and interconnected with all vehicles on the road. Moreover, Artificial Intelligence can also ease the work of police or border control in various ways. The European Union, in this context, is continuously evaluating how AI solutions can improve border controls in an ethical way. Examples of this are AI tools for better risk assessment, migration monitoring, biometric identification, or emotion detection. Other application areas of AI for law enforcement authorities include criminal investigations, preventative policing, terrorism prevention or crowd and traffic control.  

 

The SAP Business AI Approach

 

Figure 1: SAP Business AI approach

 

Artificial Intelligence is arguably at the peak of the hype phase, but AI products and solutions differ in terms of functions, modeling, and use cases. SAP in this context is focusing on using AI for improving business processes across over 25 industries, and therefore summarizes its AI products and solutions under the brand SAP Business AI. SAP Business AI is embedded into all common functional areas of companies and organizations, including human Resources, finance, procurement, and supply chain management.

In addition, SAP also launched its own Generative AI-powered Copilot, Joule, that allows customers to directly interact with SAP applications. SAP Joule can immediately answer questions, provide insights, solve tasks and create content, such as job descriptions, summaries, or SAP programming code based on simple commands. This enables users to solve tasks more quickly and run SAP systems more effectively.

SAP Business AI solutions are developed with a special emphasis on security, ethics and compliance. The basis for this is SAP’s own AI Ethics Policy, AI Ethics Steering Committee, and AI Ethics Advisory Panel. SAP in that respect was also the first European technology company to establish AI guiding principles back in 2018. The company summarizes all of these guidelines with its 3 R’s, that are:

Relevant

Relevant refers to the fact that AI is used for enhancing your business performance.

 

Reliable

Reliable means that SAP Business AI operates with integrated data from your organization or company for optimizing your business outcomes.

 

Responsible

Responsible stands for the use of the highest ethics, security, and privacy standards for all of our AI solutions.

 

Apart from that, SAP has also established a wide network of partners in the AI industry, including companies, such as, Amazon Web Services, Google, and Aleph Alpha. The aim of these partnerships is to enhance our products further, but also to provide our customers access to the best available AI services in the market.

 

Our 5 main SAP Business AI Solutions for D&S Organizations

 

1)  Force Posture and Resource Allocation with SAP Business AI

 

Figure 2: AI-based resource allocation with SAP

 

Force posture and resource allocation are critical processes for D&S organizations, regardless if within exercises or during real missions. SAP has therefore developed an AI-powered solution, that allows defense organizations to automate and optimize the matching of expected- and modelled demand and capabilities to the existing forces. This solution is likewise applicable to areas, like, military doctrine, organization, training, material, personnel, and financials. By implementing this solution, D&S organizations are enabled to raise military readiness, guarantee the most-efficient use of funds, and raise their organizational flexibility.

 

2)  Smart Procurement with SAP Business AI

 

Figure 3: SAP Business AI solutions for different procurement areas

 

Organizations in the field of Defense and Security are typically very material intensive. These organizations need to manage complex, and time critical supply chains, which usually involves a lot of manual work. SAP Business Artificial Intelligence can ease such tasks significantly by automating and speeding up these processes. The technology can be used for:

AI-powered Contract Generation

Our AI-powered solution for contract generation allows customers to create their purchase requests for diverse materials or services with just a few clicks and then automatically receive a completely pre-filled purchase contract. The responsible employees then only need to quickly check the draft contract, which enables them to save valuable time and money. In addition, it enables them to avoid potential human errors. Moreover, this solution also allows the customer to simply integrate their respective organizational guidelines and compliance rules.

The AI-powered contract generation solution is thereby built on SAP Business AI, which includes advanced Large Language Models just like Generative Artificial Intelligence and SAP’s Business Technology Platform (BTP).

 

Automated Supplier Search

Finding the right supplier from a large number of vendors can be a difficult endeavor. SAP therefore wants to help its customers to ease this process with the help of SAP Business Artificial Intelligence. Our Automated Supplier Search solution in this regard enables our customers to find, evaluate and contact the right supplier for every command in an automated way. By implementing this solution, D&S organizations can speed up their procurement processes and make them more efficient.

 

Further AI-based Functions for Procurement

SAP, beyond that, offers a wide range of additional AI features for procurement. This includes automated category management, delivery-date predictions, invoice and payment management just like supplier management or the recruitment of external workforces.

 

3)  Accelerated Engineering Information Handling with SAP Business AI

 

Figure 4: Exemplary presentation of Accelerated Engineering Information Handling with SAP Business AI

 

Military-, but also different emergency organizations usually need to handle large volumes of technical documentation for their equipment. This process involves the registration, categorization, control and maintenance as well as the provision of engineering documents. The organizations usually receive these documents from their suppliers like, for instance, military system integrators and then have to manually process them. In order to overcome these challenges, SAP created an innovative software solution, that is called Accelerated Engineering Information Handling. With this solution, that is based on SAP Business AI, our customers are enabled to automate and optimize the complete engineering information handling process. This not only allows them to reduce costs and process time, but also to improve force readiness. The Accelerated Engineering Handling solution thereby not solely speeds up and eases the process, but also allows the user to manage the data sets in an easy and comprehensive manner. This, as the below graphic shows, also includes the ability of measuring the data quality and identify incorrect and incomplete data.­ 

 ­­

Figure 5: Example illustration of data quality evaluation process with SAP

  

4)  Humanitarian Response with SAP Business AI

 

Figure 6: United Nations humanitarian aid camp

 

Humanitarian aid missions are typically extremely time critical, which requires immediate action by all members of the humanitarian aid organization and other stakeholders. Especially worth mentioning here are supply chains for medicine or food. SAP for this reason created the SAP 4W-Wizard tool, a software solution, that allows to better manage disaster relief missions. The 4W-Wizard tool is based on SAP Business AI and allows to completely digitize and better coordinate different forms of aid missions. The solution, that is based on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), enables aid organizations to easily create supply chain collaboration platforms. The SAP 4W-Wizard tool has been successfully implemented by the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (UN OCHA), which used the application for different rescue missions in the Asia Pacific region, including countries like Nepal, Vietnam, the Philippines, South Sudan or Turkey. The implementation of the software solution allowed UN OCHA to achieve a 100% auditability and digitization of its aid operations. In addition, the aid organization was able to achieve time savings of more than 99%.

 

5)  Forecasting and Managing Extreme Weather Events with SAP Business AI

 

Figure 7: Assessment of landslide risk by region with SAP EDiSON Dashboard

 

As the recent temperature record for April of more than 30 degrees in European countries like Austria, Germany or the Czech Republic have shown, extreme weather events and natural disasters, such as heat waves, earthquakes, floods, or landslides, are forecast to occur more frequently and intensely due to climate change. This development poses a severe threat for people around the world, and here in particular for the ones living in developing countries. In response to these threatening developments, SAP and the Japanese Oita University successfully established a partnership for the creation of a response collaboration tool. This software tool is called EDiSON Dashboard (an abbreviation for Earth Disaster Intelligent System Operational Network) and enables weather organizations, road operators, railway operators just like national, regional, and local governments or aid organizations to better forecast and manage extreme weather events. The EDiSON Dashboard thereby includes functions, such as:

  • Risk Assessment for Road Traffic
  • Risk Assessment for Rail Traffic
  • Assessment of Landslide Risk by Region
  • Soil Precipitation Index and Risk Analysis
  • Landslide Risk for Elderly Population
  • Chronology of Events

 

SAP EDiSON at its core analyzes multiple data, such as, real-time data from diverse sensors, weather forecasts and historical weather data and processes it with the help of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning technology. The EDiSON software application therewith can perform real-time disaster-risk evaluations and create precise forecasts. These forecasts then can be used for initiating preventive measures or timely evacuation orders. EDiSON has been successfully applied by the very earthquake-prone Japanese Oita Prefecture, and here allowed to create a 40% increase in data granularity of disaster-response risk compared to traditional methods. In addition, the SAP EDiSON Dashboard is capable of creating precise forecasts for up to 15 hours. It beyond that can create automated early warnings in case if a certain event, such as rising water levels, occurs.

 

Additional AI Solutions from Partners and SAP in the SAP Store

 

Apart from the above-mentioned five core solutions, SAP also offers several helpful AI applications from selected partner companies and SAP itself directly in the SAP Store. Applications, that are of special interest for D&S organizations are, for instance:

 

ERPsim for SAP S/4HANA

 

 

Figure 8: ERPsim simulation for Defense and Security organizations

 

With ERPsim by Baton Simulations Inc., companies and organizations can run different business simulations on a live SAP S/4HANA system in a fun way. ERPsim is based on AI technology and can be used for trainings, exercises or onboarding.

 

Decision Int­­elligence for Military Readiness

 

Figure 9: Decision Intelligence for Military Readiness by 4CAST Systems LTD

Decision Intelligence for Military Readiness is an AI-powered software solution, that allows military organizations to train and improve military readiness. The solution, that was developed by 4CAST Systems LTD, thereby does not only allow to measure, adjust, and forecast readiness, but also provides helpful suggestions for improvement to the exercise participants.

 

FieldTekPro - Intelligent Mobile Asset Maintenance

 

Figure 10: Intelligent Mobile Asset Maintenance with FieldTekPro

FieldTekPro by Enstrapp IT Solutions Pvt Ltd uses AI for improving asset and plant maintenance operations. By applying FieldTekPro, D&S organizations can reduce asset downtime and increase technician productivity, while also reducing the amount of paperwork.

 

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

 

Figure 11: SAP Enterprise Threat Detection

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection is an additional software package, that includes advanced security features and add-ons for SAP systems. It allows D&S organizations to monitor and enhance the security of SAP applications and protects them from potential cybersecurity threats.

 

The Metaverse Solution for Enterprise Dual Carbon

 

Figure 12: The metaverse solution for enterprise dual carbon by iSoftStone

The metaverse solution for enterprise dual carbon is an advanced, AI-backed energy emission management software, that allows to monitor and steer emissions in real-time. The solution, that was created by iSoftStone Information Technology (Group) Company Limited also entails a sophisticated graphical user interface, with detailed graphical representations.

 

iSoftStone Dual-Carbon Solutions for Buildings

 

Figure 13: iSoftStone’s Dual-Carbon Solutions for Buildings

The iSoftStone Dual-Carbon Solutions for Buildings is an additional AI solution by SoftStone Information Technology (Group) Company Limited. It allows D&S organizations to measure, monitor and reduce emissions of buildings. The SAP partner solution also includes advanced, three-dimensional graphic animations as well as the ability to build energy-saving and emission-reducing applications with it.

 

 2BM Mobile Warehouse

 

Figure 14: 2BM Mobile Warehouse app

2BM Mobile Warehouse was developed by 2BM A/S, and is an AI-powered solution for improving warehouse scanning. The application allows for a better warehouse scanning experience by combining advanced barcode scanning technology with a state-of-the-art user interface and full integration into SAP warehousing systems. 

 

GMetri

 

Figure 15: Demo of metaverse-base onboarding with GMetri

GMetri by GMETRI Inc. with offers D&S organizations a revolutionary, no-code metaverse platform for employee onboarding. By implementing GMetri, D&S organizations can not only bring their onboarding process to a new level, but also offer their employees a truly unique onboarding experience, that is entirely time- and location-independent. 

 

Land Displacement Monitoring 

 

Figure 16: Example of a land displacement analysis

Land Displacement Monitoring was created by Synspective Inc., and allows Defense and Security, and here especially civil protection organizations, to better monitor, detect and analyze land displacement. The solution works based on millimeter precise satellite technology and therefore can be applied even in areas, that are normally not accessible for measuring engineers.

 

Key Takeaways

 

To sum up, it can be said that SAP Business Artificial Intelligence does sustainably improve the Defense and Security industry by automating and optimizing various business processes. Good examples for this are the SAP Joule Copilot, which helps the user to solve diverse tasks within SAP applications faster and easier, or AI functions and features, that are integrated into SAP applications for functional processes like human resource management or procurement. In this way D&S organizations are enabled to save valuable time and money, that they can instead use for their core tasks. Apart from this, the blog entry also showed that SAP offers a great variety of additional AI solutions and partner solutions within the SAP Store.

 

Sources

 

Total global AI investment 2015-2022 | Statista. (2023, April). Statista.

Anduril Raises $1.48 Billion in Series E Funding | by Anduril Industries | Anduril Blog. (2022, December). Anduril.

Artificial intelligence at EU borders: Overview of applications and key issues. (2021, July, 07). European Parliament.

Czech Republic Sees First Tropical Day of 2024, With Temperatures of 30.9°C – Brno Daily. (2024, April). Brno Daily.

Germany basks in record April heat as spring temperatures soar (yahoo.com). (2024, April). Yahoo.

How AI is establishing itself as the newest public safety officer. (2020, January, 31). EY New Zealand.

How Technology Can Help Meet Rising Military Demand (forbes.com). (2024, March). Forbes.

entry.title: Shape the Future of SAP Concur with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative: Discover New Features
entry.content:
Discover the power of SAP Concur, a cutting-edge solution that connects financial data and empowers businesses to take control of their spending. By automating spending processes, SAP Concur enables your business to run efficiently, anytime and anywhere, even in the most challenging circumstances. 
 
SAP Concur is constantly evolving, and you have the opportunity to shape its future. Scroll through the list of SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects for SAP Concur and get a sneak peek at the planned new features and functions. These projects cover a wide range of areas, from pre-booking approval for travel and request to company notes on the SAP Concur homepage as well as Joule. 
 
To participate in any of these projects, make sure to register on the Customer Influence website by July 12, 2024. Your participation will not only give you a voice in shaping the future of SAP Concur but also provide you with early insights into new features and functions. 
 
So, what are you waiting for?  

SAP Concur Solutions projects for registration: 

  • Concur Expense - "Expense Reimagined" Concept 
  • Concur Travel and Concur Request - Pre-booking approval 
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Company Notes on the SAP Concur Homepage 
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Concur Home Page Redesign 
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Extensibility and External Integration 
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Ideas for Future of Travel and Expense Card Spend Controls 
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Joule (Digital Assistant) 
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Support Knowledge Base Articles- Customer Experience and Feedback  
  • SAP Concur Solutions – The Future of Data Delivery Service 
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Accruals Handling 
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Payroll integration 

Don't delay – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2024!  If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here. 

Warm regards, 

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Asset Collaboration - Automatically Share Work Orders
entry.content:
Why Share Work Orders Anyway?

The SAP Business Network for Asset Collaboration (BNAC) removes the need for emailing or extracting work orders to excel and instead lets your partners work from a single version of the truth. What are the key reasons that organisations share work orders?

Sharing Work Orders with Service / Maintenance Providers enables:

  • Review of the planned work and associated assets, work history, and documentation
  • Collaboration on the scope & duration of the work
  • Acceptance of the work
  • Confirmation that the work is complete
  • Confirmation of the operation job times
  • The ability to create follow-on notifications

Sharing work orders with OEMs & regulators enables:

  • Provide feedback on Asset Performance
  • Add details & depth to warranty claims
  • Reporting work performed by third parties

In addition multiple parties might be involved in work order planning or scheduling, often with different partners responsible for different operations. Using the SAP Business Network means all partners gain secure access to the details they need.

 

How to setup automatic sharing?

The SAP Business Network for Asset Collaboration can automatically share work orders with a business partner that is specified on the Work Order operation in the S/4HANA or ECC system. The following points will help ensure that you are setup and ready to share.

 

Enable Automatic Sharing in BNAC

Automatic Sharing must be enabled in your companies application settings:

 

Automatic Sharing with Partners in BNAC

It's straightforward to enable automatic sharing between your company. At a high level the prerequisites and steps are:

  • Your company must be connected with the business partner in BNAC
  • Your company must have the business partner from your ERP mapped to the BNAC business partner
  • You must set-up an automatic sharing pattern for the business partner

To help clarify lets recap the BNAC business partner fundamentals. The SAP Business Network needs to understand the relationship between Business Partners on your ERP (e.g. S/4HANA), Business Partners on the SAP Business Network, and Business Partners of other connected parties. The following diagram gives a high level overview..

 

Connecting your company to the Business Partner in BNAC

To be able to Map a business partner Your Company must have connected to the BNAC Business Partner (e.g. BNAC- ABC Services). Check this (or connect) via the Business Partners tile of the Administration Tab….

 

Mapping Business Partners from your ERP to BNAC business partners

Your company has an ERP e.g. SAP S/4HANA with business partners (for example you have a service provider known as ABC Services).

Your company joins the SAP Business Network for Asset Collaboration and BNAC represents the business partners from your ERP as External Organizations

You can see these in BNAC by navigating to the Administration tab and selecting the Company Profile tile…

Once on the Company Profile application select External Organizations…

The SAP Business Network for Asset Collaboration (BNAC) has business partners that have joined the network. This may for example include the service provider ABC Services.

However BNAC does not yet understand that the service provider ABC Services belonging to Your Company is the same as the ABC Services on BNAC.

To map the business partner from Your Company to BNAC select the External Organization to display its profile….

Then Select “Map Business Partner”

In the pop-up select the BNAC Business Partner that maps to the Your Company external organization (e.g. BNAC – ABC Services will be mapped to Your Company ABC Services).

Now BNAC understands that Your Company partner ABC Services is the same as the BNAC partner BNAC – ABC Services 😊

 

Create Automatic Sharing Criteria

Once the relationships are understood then it is possible to define the automatic sharing criteria. Use the Network Authorizations tile in the Administration tab and select Patterns….

Create a new pattern…

 

Add the Business Partner

 

Now you have a rule in place that will automatically share Work Orders whenever they are assigned to the Business Partner (vendor) on the Work Order operation. 

Example S/4HANA Work Order with a PM03 Operation

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start – Smartwatch Companion Deep Dive
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, staying connected and efficient on the go is crucial. SAP Mobile Start, our native mobile entry point, let’s you integrate business processes and content to provide users with an intuitive and powerful experience right at their fingertips. To enhance productivity and accessibility even further, SAP has expanded its capabilities with the Smartwatch Companion app, with the release of 1.6 version. 

Now, over a year later with the current release at 1.12, multiple features have been added to the Smartwatch Companion. It’s time to provide you with a comprehensive overview of what the Watch Application offers. So, in this blog, we will explore the features and functionalities of the SAP Mobile Start Smartwatch Companion and see how it can bring valuable business information and workflow insights to your wrist. 

How to enable the Smartwatch Companion via SAP Mobile Services?  

By default, the Smartwatch Companion is disabled and needs to manually be activated by the admin. This is done similar to other options and security features via the SAP Mobile Services Cockpit for SAP Mobile Start:  

  1. Access the SAP Mobile Services Cockpit for SAP Mobile Start: 
    In short: two steps are required. First (if not already done) you need to create a Role Collection “MobileTenantAdmin” that holds the correct MobileAdmin role and assign it to your user. Last you need to construct the Admin Cockpits URL and open it in your browser.  

    https://<tenant-subdomain>.m.launchpad.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/mobileservices/admin 

    You can find a more detailed description on our help page Admin Cockpit Access Control.

  2. Find and activate the Smartwatch Feature Flag:

    Inside the Admin Cockpit navigate to “Mobile Settings Exchange” and scroll down to the “Feature Flags” section. Within the table you will find the various feature flags offered for the SAP Mobile Start. You can find out more about them here. 
    To enable the Smartwatch Companion, locate the line item called “Smartwatch” and set the Slider Button in the Active Column to “On”: 

    Make sure to Save afterwards. 

  3. (Optional) Enable Passcode Requirement: 
    You might have noticed the second Watch-related feature flag “SmartwatchPasscodeRequired”. This is an additional security feature that can be enabled to require end-users to passcode-protect their watches before using SAP Mobile Start on their watch.  

 

Features

Next to the standard watchOS (iOS) and Wear OS (Android) feature to receive native notifications (from SAP Mobile Start) on your wrist, the companion app for SAP Mobile Start offers additional features. At the current state of SAP Mobile Start 1.12 they can be divided into two different categories: 

Complications & in-app browsing and actions for Monitoring Apps and To-Dos. 

Let’s have a closer look one by one.  


SAP Mobile Start Complications:  

Watch complications are additional features offered as part of a watch face extending it to not only show the time. In smartwatches they are customizable UI elements that can provide a variety of information. SAP Mobile Start offers additional business-related complications to put on your watch face, so you can see insights directly from your wrist.  

There’s the monitoring complication which displays info directly from monitoring tiles and 3 types of complications for To-Dos (All, High Priority, with due date):  

Managing watch faces and complications is different between the operating systems and available watches. You can learn more about complications and how to use them via:  

watchOS ComplicationsWear OS Complications
  • Developer Guide 
  • Developer Guide
  • User Guide 
  • User Guide (for Pixel watch)

In-App browsing for Monitoring apps and To-Dos: 

With complications only limited information is available directly via the watch face. To get more insights and even act on items you need to open the SAP Mobile Start’s watch app.  

Opening the app, you will get two main screens: Monitoring and To-Dos. You can switch between them by swiping left/right. Both offer a list of either the To-Dos or Monitoring apps that are assigned to you. It looks like this:  

The To-Dos screen additionally offers filter options visible at the top of the screen. With the available buttons the To-Dos can be filtered by “High-Priority”, “Due Soon” and “Quick Actions”.  

While the Monitoring section is mostly for viewing the related KPIs, To-Dos can be acted on directly from the watch. If you see the 3 dots menu on a To-Do you can immediately jump into quick actions (e.g. Approve / Decline) from there. Otherwise clicking a To-Do from the list will display a details section you can scroll through for more information. Also from here you can directly take quick actions and even add comments to your decision via e.g. voice input: 


Note: 
Currently only supported for Wear OS it’s possible to perform a “handoff” to your phone. It can be done by clicking on a monitoring tile in the Monitoring section or clicking the “Open on Phone” button within the To-Do Details view. This action will open SAP Mobile Start and even the related component (Monitoring Tile / To-Do) within the app on your phone. 

 

Additional Information

For further and always up-to-date information about the features and applying restrictions please refer to our SAP Mobile Start help guides:  

  • Admin Guide – Watch Support 
  • User Guide – Watch App 

That concludes my post. I hope you learned something about the SAP Mobile Start watch companion that you did not know yet! Let me know if you make use of it already, if not, give it a try! 

Stay up to date with the latest news and post your questions or feedback about SAP Mobile Start in the Q&A area. Start by visiting your SAP Mobile Experience community page and click “Follow”. We’ll be publishing more informative blog posts. Want to be notified? Check your #communicationsto ensure you have your notifications activated.

entry.title: Introducing SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager - Your bridge to successful transformations
entry.content:
Introducing SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

“Information is power” - we've all heard the saying. However, when it comes to business transformation, information alone is not enough, it’s overwhelming! The power to change comes from having the right toolset to manage information, curate it, and convert it into meaningful and actionable change. 

Earlier this month at SAP Sapphire, we announced the latest addition to our SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite: SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager!  

This comprehensive transformation management toolkit is designed to empower the people who are driving change within their organizations. It facilitates collaborative management of process insights and improvement initiatives, supporting value-driven decision-making and establishing performance baselines for measurable success. 

It was truly exciting to see our new product in action on stage during Juergen Mueller’s keynote and showcased in the central showcase, especially knowing the immense hard work, innovation, and dedication our teams invested. Witnessing our customers’ enthusiastic response was indeed rewarding. 

 

Why SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager? 

In today’s rapidly evolving business landscapes, there are two levers that impact an organization's ability to transform effectively: how quickly they can gather knowledge about improvement opportunities and how seamlessly this knowledge can be funneled and translated into change.  

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager serves as the control center for your transformation initiatives. The solution enables:  

  • Centralized and collaborative improvements: drive strategic and tactical initiatives collaboratively from a single place within SAP Signavio. Leverage pre-defined and problem-centric content to accelerate value and outcomes. 
  • Value-driven decision-making: understand the real value potential behind identified improvement opportunities and the ability to set informed goals by benchmarking against industry standards and leading peers.  
  • Quantifiable value through continuous improvement: ensure effective prioritization, funnel management, and monitor outcomes for sustained success. 

 

Capabilities included in the Initial Release 

With our initial release in May 2024, we have enabled the first set of capabilities designed to collaboratively manage improvement initiatives and set realistic targets for the key performance indicators targeted by those initiatives:  

Centralized Insights and Initiatives 

  • Capture and consolidate knowledge and data points from SAP Signavio and other sources into one central place. 
  • Eliminate siloed information, ensuring all stakeholders have one single place to drive change. 
  • Collaborate on, prioritize, and manage process improvement efforts efficiently. 

Benchmarking Analytics 

  • Set baseline of your company's performance relative to industry standards and leading peers. 
  • Identify improvement areas quickly and apply various filters that segment the displayed benchmarks. 

For more information on what was released, please check out our detailed May 2024 Release blog here. 

 

What’s Next? 

Here is a glimpse of the features that are planned to come later this year: 

Opportunity Discovery 

Value Analysis  

Task Management 

Monitoring 

Discover available templates for improvement initiative based on a wide range of processes and systems. 

Estimate the monetary impact of discovered inefficiencies and related value of the improvement opportunities. 

Manage the day-to-day work of transformation initiatives, align with key stakeholders, and collaborate across the company. 

Track and prove the delivered value and organizational impact of transformation initiatives. 

 

As the latest addition to our solution portfolio, SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager is the new standard to support organizations in their quest for continuous improvement and operational excellence.  

We look forward to seeing it in action on a wide range of use cases! 

 

How to Get Started! 

As part of the initial launch, we invite you to explore the capabilities of SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager and experience this new offering, starting June 2024. 

We are excited to offer places in our Early Adopter Care Program (EACP) for organizations eager to get started with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager straight away. Participants in this program will receive dedicated support, including onboarding, training, and access to our product teams to ensure success and drive enhanced product experience and capabilities.  

The EACP is designed to ensure our early adopters achieve maximum value and success from our new solution, while also helping us refine and enhance the product based on real-world usage and insights. Participation in the program is confirmed based on a selection process including an assessment of the proposed use case and project scope. 

For more information or to express your interest, please contact your account executive or customer support manager. And of course, you can also sign up for the EACP directly.  

 

What are your biggest challenges in managing transformation initiatives? Let me know your thoughts below!

entry.title: Virtual Summit: Customer use cases in a hybrid environment
entry.content:
In today’s digital age, many organizations are operating in a hybrid IT environment, with a mix of on-premises systems and cloud-based applications and infrastructure. Managing and connecting these disparate landscapes can be a complex task, but it’s essential for businesses to effectively leverage their technology investments to stay competitive.

 

SAP Integration Suite’s new hybrid runtime, Edge Integration Cell, is designed to help organizations seamlessly manage their on-premises and cloud landscapes. This innovative solution enables businesses to connect to on-premises systems in their private landscapes, as well as to remote locations such as warehouses and remote facilities. Additionally, it provides support for migrating from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite, and for integrating with IoT devices and sensors in remote locations.

 

One common use case for Edge Integration Cell is connecting to on-premises systems in private landscapes. Many organizations have legacy systems and applications that are located on-premises, and need to be connected to their cloud-based environments. Edge Integration Cell makes it easy to bridge the gap between these different landscapes, allowing for seamless integration and data exchange. Moreover, Edge Integration Cell also supports the migration from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite. As businesses evolve and upgrade their IT landscapes, having a seamless migration path is essential for ensuring continuity and minimizing disruption. This new hybrid runtime facilitates a smooth transition to SAP Integration Suite, allowing organizations to modernize their integration capabilities without any downtime.

 

Additionally, Edge Integration Cell allows integration with IoT devices and sensors in remote locations. With the increasing adoption of IoT technology, many businesses are looking to leverage data from sensors and devices in their operations. Edge Integration Cell provides the necessary infrastructure and tools to connect and integrate with these IoT devices, enabling organizations to harness valuable insights and drive innovation.

 

Lastly, managing IT landscapes in a hybrid environment is a critical challenge for many organizations. However, with solutions like SAP Integration Suite’s Edge Integration Cell, businesses can effectively bridge their on-premises and cloud landscapes and unlock new opportunities for innovation and growth.

 

Hear how Edge Integration Cell, the new hybrid runtime for SAP Integration Suite, helps you manage and bridge your on-remises and cloud landscapes.

entry.title: Advancing to Multi-task Multilayer Perceptron: a new Neural Network design in SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

As one of the most advanced machine learning techniques, neural networks excel in handling complex data patterns and making predictions with high accuracy. Neural networks have shown state-of-the-art capabilities in many classification and regression tasks. In the previous release of Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), a function using neural networks called multilayer perceptron (PAL_MULTILAYER_PERCEPTRON) has been provided. From the standpoint of user experience and keeping pace with advancements in neural network technology, this previous function implementation however sometimes falls short in meeting customer performance expectations and requirements for multi-task learning. Therefore, we have introduced a new neural network function, e.g., a Multi-task Multilayer Perceptron (PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK) in SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q2 release. Compared to the original function, the new function will provide improvements in the following areas:

  1. Supports built-in multi-task classification scenario
  2. Enhanced early-stop method to avoid model-overfitting issue in neural networks
  3. Fix output activations for easier parameter tuning and better user experience.
  4. More network complexity control techniques and optimization methods.

Multi-Task Learning

Multi-task learning (MTL) in multilayer perceptrons involves training a single neural network to perform multiple related tasks at once. It utilizes shared hidden layers to capture common features across tasks and task-specific output layers for unique predictions. The network is jointly optimized for all tasks, which can enhance generalization and lead to better performance than training separate networks. MTL is efficient as it reduces the total number of parameters needed. It also facilitates the transfer of knowledge between tasks, which is particularly beneficial when tasks are related. Multi-task classification and multi-task regression have a wide range of applications. For instance, multi-task classification can be used for automated multi-field value proposals or pre-filling of forms, such as Sales Order Automation. Multi-task regression can be used for predicting multiple price or sales targets within a single model. 

In prior PAL_MULTILAYER_PERCEPTRON function, multi-task learning is only supported in regression scenario. In PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK function, multi-task learning is supported in both classification and regression tasks.

Example

INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('DEPENDENT_VARIABLE', NULL, NULL, 'LABEL1');

INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('DEPENDENT_VARIABLE', NULL, NULL, 'LABEL2');

To use this feature, user needs to define all the targets by DEPENDENT_VARIABLE parameter.

Early Stop Method

Early stopping is a regularization technique used in training neural networks to prevent overfitting and to hasten the training process. It is a practical and effective method to enhance the generalization capabilities of neural networks by avoiding excessive training that can lead to overfitting. It involves monitoring the model's performance on a validation set and stopping the training process when the performance begins to degrade or stops improving. The following graph shows how early stop method works in neural networks:

 

The input data is partitioned into training and validation subsets based on the specified TRAINING_PERCENTAGE. The designated training subset is utilized for model development, while the validation subset serves to assess the model's performance, providing a measure of validation loss at the conclusion of each training epoch. The training process will be terminated if the predefined stopping criteria are satisfied, which are governed by the parameters outlined below:

  • EARLY_STOP: specifies whether to use the auto early stopping method. If don’t use, training would stop after MAX_ITERATION is reached.
  • WARMUP_EPOCHS: number of epochs to wait before executing the auto early stopping method.
  • PATIENCE: Number of epochs to wait before ending the training if no improvement is shown.
  • MAX_ITERATION: maximum number of training epochs.

Besides, SAVE_BEST_MODEL parameter can be used to specifies whether to save your model's weights with minimum loss during training.

Optimized Parameter Configuration 

The primary focus of the updates is on the parameters HIDDEN_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC and OUTPUT_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC. For HIDDEN_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC, several unsuitable and obsolete activation functions, such as linear activations, have been phased out.

The parameter OUTPUT_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC has been deprecated in the context of multi-task multilayer perceptrons. In classification tasks, the softmax activation function is now standard for the output layer, whereas a linear activation is employed for regression tasks.

Optimization Methods

An optimizer is an algorithm that adjusts the weights of the network in order to minimize a loss function. The choice of optimizer can have a significant impact on the performance and convergence speed of the network.

Consequently, in addition to the Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) provided in the prior neural network functions, we have now incorporated more widely used optimizers, such as RMSprop, Adam, and AdaGrad.

Example

INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('OPTIMIZER', 2, NULL, NULL);

Network Complexity Control Techniques

Batch Normalization

Batch normalization is a technique that significantly accelerates neural network training by stabilizing the distribution of inputs to layers, which helps in achieving faster convergence. It reduces the model's sensitivity to weight initialization, allowing for more efficient training from various starting points and also enables the use of higher learning rates. Additionally, batch normalization provides a regularization effect, which can help in generalizing the model better and reducing the likelihood of overfitting.

Example

INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('USE_BATCHNORM', 1, NULL, NULL);

Dropout

Dropout is a powerful regularization technique that prevents overfitting in neural networks by randomly deactivating a subset of neurons during training, which encourages the network to develop more robust features. This method promotes redundancy in the network, as it ensures that no single neuron can dominate the learning process, thereby improving the model's ability to generalize from the training data to unseen data.

Example:

INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES (' DROPOUT_PROB', NULL, 0.1, NULL);

Efficiency and Accuracy Measures

In this section, we present the time cost and accuracy outcomes derived from our evaluations on various classification and regression datasets.

Classification

 Cover Type dataset

Cover Type dataset  is part of the UCI Machine Learning Repository, contains tree observations from four areas of the Roosevelt National Forest in Colorado. This dataset has 581012 instances, containing 54 features and a target. 15120 of them are using for training, the remaining are used for validation.

Function Name

Parameter configuration

Metric on Evaluation Data

Time cost

PAL_MULTILAYER_PERCEPTRON

hidden_layer_size: [60] * 3

activation: relu

output_activation: linear

training_style: batch

learning_rate: 0.01

max_iteration: 500

 

  

 

Accuracy: 74.03%

 

Training:

444.9s

PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK

hidden_layer_size: [60] * 3

batch_size: 1024

max_iteration: 500

patience: 5

training_percentage: 1.0

learning_rate: 0.005

 

Accuracy: 74.5%

Training:

94.7s

Code Sample

 

 

SET SCHEMA DM_PAL; 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_CLS_PRE_MODEL_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_CLS_PRE_MODEL_TBL( 
	"ROW_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"PART_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"MODEL_CONTENT" NVARCHAR(5000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL( 
	"ROW_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"PART_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"MODEL_CONTENT" NVARCHAR(5000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL ( 
	"ITERATION" INTEGER,  
	"ERROR" DOUBLE 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL ( 
	"STAT_NAME" NVARCHAR(256),  
	"STAT_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_PARAMETER_TBL( 
	"PARAM_NAME" NVARCHAR(256), 
	"INT_VALUE" INTEGER, 
	"DOUBLE_VALUE" DOUBLE, 
	"STRING_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HAS_ID', 1, NULL, Null); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HIDDEN_LAYER_SIZE', NULL, NULL, '60, 60, 60'); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('MAX_ITERATION', 1000, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HIDDEN_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC', 2, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('EARLY_STOP', 1, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('PATIENCE', 5, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('LEARNING_RATE', NULL, 0.005, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('DEPENDENT_VARIABLE', NULL, NULL, 'LABEL'); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('BATCH_SIZE', 1024, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('NORMALIZATION', 1, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('SEED', 1234, NULL, NULL); 
DO BEGIN 
	lt_data = SELECT * FROM PAL_TRAIN_COVERTYPE_DATA_TBL; 
	lt_param = SELECT * FROM PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
	lt_premodel = SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_CLS_PRE_MODEL_TBL; 
	CALL _SYS_AFL.PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK(:lt_data, :lt_param, :lt_premodel, lt_model, lt_log, lt_stat, lt_opt, lt_ph); 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_model; 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_log; 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_stat; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL;	 
END; 

DROP TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY COLUMN TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL( 
	"PARAM_NAME" NVARCHAR(256), 
	"INT_VALUE" INTEGER, 
	"DOUBLE_VALUE" DOUBLE, 
	"STRING_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
CALL _SYS_AFL.PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK_PREDICT(PAL_PREDICT_COVERTYPE_DATA_TBL, "#PAL_PARAMETER_TBL", PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL, ?, ?);

 

 

 

 

Regression

Appliance Energy dataset 

Appliance Energy dataset is also from the UCI Machine Learning Repository, whhich includes variables related to home appliances, weather patterns, and other environmental elements. Its main goal is to predict a household's energy usage based on the input features.  This dataset has 19735 instances, containing 28 features and a target. 75% of them are using for training, 25% are used for validation.

Function Name

Parameter configuration

Metric on Evaluation Data

Time cost

PAL_MULTILAYER_PERCEPTRON

hidden_layer_size: [100]

activation: relu

output_activation: linear

training_style: batch

max_iteration: 1000

learning_rate: 0.001

 

  

 

R2 ~ 0.42

Training:

218.9s

PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK

hidden_layer_size: [100] * 2

batch_size: 1024

max_iteration: 500

patience: 5

training_percentage: 1.0

learning_rate: 0.01

 

R2 ~ 0.44

Training: 54s

Code Sample

 

 

SET SCHEMA DM_PAL; 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_REG_PRE_MODEL_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_REG_PRE_MODEL_TBL( 
	"ROW_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"PART_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"MODEL_CONTENT" NVARCHAR(5000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_REG_MODEL_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_REG_MODEL_TBL( 
	"ROW_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"PART_INDEX" INTEGER, 
	"MODEL_CONTENT" NVARCHAR(5000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL( 
	"ITERATION" INTEGER,  
	"ERROR" DOUBLE 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL ( 
	"STAT_NAME" NVARCHAR(256),  
	"STAT_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
DROP TABLE PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
CREATE COLUMN TABLE PAL_PARAMETER_TBL( 
	"PARAM_NAME" NVARCHAR(256), 
	"INT_VALUE" INTEGER, 
	"DOUBLE_VALUE" DOUBLE, 
	"STRING_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HAS_ID', 1, NULL, Null); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('FUNCTIONALITY', 1, NULL, Null); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('BATCH_SIZE', 1024, NULL, Null); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HIDDEN_LAYER_SIZE', NULL, NULL, '100, 100'); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('HIDDEN_LAYER_ACTIVE_FUNC', 2, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('MAX_ITERATION', 1000, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('LEARNING_RATE', NULL, 0.2, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('SEED', 1234, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('PATIENCE', 5, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('OPTIMIZER', 2, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('LEARNING_RATE', NULL, 0.01, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('TRAINING_PERCENTAGE', NULL, 1, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('NORMALIZATION', 1, NULL, NULL); 
INSERT INTO PAL_PARAMETER_TBL VALUES ('DEPENDENT_VARIABLE', NULL, NULL, 'Appliances'); 
DO BEGIN 
	lt_data = SELECT * FROM PAL_TRAIN_ENERGY_DATA_TBL; 
	lt_param = SELECT * FROM PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
	lt_premodel = SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_REG_PRE_MODEL_TBL; 
	CALL _SYS_AFL.PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK(:lt_data, :lt_param, :lt_premodel, lt_model, lt_log, lt_stat, lt_opt, lt_ph); 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_REG_MODEL_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_model; 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_log; 
	INSERT INTO PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL SELECT * FROM :lt_stat; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_REG_MODEL_TBL; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_LOG_TBL; 
	SELECT * FROM PAL_MLP_STAT_TBL;	 
END; 
DROP TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL; 
CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY COLUMN TABLE #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL( 
	"PARAM_NAME" NVARCHAR(256), 
	"INT_VALUE" INTEGER, 
	"DOUBLE_VALUE" DOUBLE, 
	"STRING_VALUE" NVARCHAR(1000) 
); 
CALL _SYS_AFL.PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK_PREDICT(PAL_PREDICT_ENERGY_DATA_TBL, "#PAL_PARAMETER_TBL", PAL_MLP_CLS_MODEL_TBL, ?, ?);

 

 

From the results, it is evident that the new neural network implementation has achieved better evaluation metric results and faster processing speeds.

Summary

In comparison to previous neural network implementations, PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK now supports multi-task learning, adeptly handling both classification and regression tasks. It introduces an early stopping method designed to mitigate the challenges of underfitting and overfitting. The incorporation of fixed activation parameters streamlines the process of parameter tuning, enhancing the overall user experience. Furthermore, PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK offers an expanded selection of optimizers and advanced network control techniques, providing users with greater flexibility and control in their modeling endeavors. Lastly, PAL_MLP_MULTI_TASK has demonstrated superior performance, delivering improved evaluation outcomes and accelerated training speeds on our validation datasets.

Useful Links

  • Install the Python Machine Learning client for SAP HANA from the pypi public repository: hana-ml
  • HANA Predictive Analysis Library Documentation
  • Other blog posts on hana-ml: 

Global Explanation Capabilities in SAP HANA Machine Learning

Exploring ML Explainability in SAP HANA PAL – Classification and Regression

Fairness in Machine Learning - A New Feature in SAP HANA Cloud PAL

A Multivariate Time Series Modeling and Forecasting Guide with Python Machine Learning Client for SA...

Outlier Detection using Statistical Tests in Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA

Outlier Detection by Clustering using Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA

Anomaly Detection in Time-Series using Seasonal Decomposition in Python Machine Learning Client for ...

Outlier Detection with One-class Classification using Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA

Learning from Labeled Anomalies for Efficient Anomaly Detection using Python Machine Learning Client...

Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA

Import multiple excel files into a single SAP HANA table

COPD study, explanation and interpretability with Python machine learning client for SAP HANA

entry.title: Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
entry.content:
Blog Series
Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 1 - Concept
Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 2 - Building a Text Summarizer
Integrating AI with SAPUI5 Fiori Apps: Part 3 - Building an AI Chatbot Assistant

With the announcement of SAP Generative AI Hub, there has been an explosion of interest in exploring how artificial intelligence can change how enterprise applications are developed and consumed. The official BTP Reference Architecture for GenAI and RAG provides guidance on how to build full-stack apps integrating with LLMs via SAP AI Core. Many times, all you need is to integrate your freestyle or Fiori Elements SAPUI5 apps quickly with LLMs for use cases where you need GenAI - LLM integration only on the web client side.

Some of the use cases where a client-side LLM integration within SAPUI5 apps are needed include, but are not limited to:

  1. Product Description Generator
  2. Job Description Generator
  3. Customer Query Summarizer
  4. Campaign Idea/Title Generator
  5. AI Assistant Chatbot (without RAG—to answer based on a limited set of instructions)

I'm beginning this blog in this series by outlining an approach using SAP BTP Destinations to achieve LLM integration easily and securely, for deployment and development. The techniques outlined can be used right away in a productive setting. In the subsequent blogs, I'll walk you through an overview of how to build a simple Text Summarizer and a slightly advanced AI Chatbot Assistant.

Below is the solution to how this works. Please note that Standalone Approuter is optional for consumption via SAP Workzone or directly via SAP HTML5 Repository since both have SAP Managed Approuter running behind the scenes and would respect the xs-app.json configuration.

With this context set, let's get started to make your SAPUI5 apps AI-enabled ✨!

1. Create an SAPUI5 app

If you don't already have a SAPUI5 app (Freestyle or Fiori Elements), you can create a new app via SAP Build Code (Create > Build an Application > SAP Build Code > SAP Fiori Application) or in VS Code using SAP Fiori Tools Extensions.

2. Create a Destination for LLM deployment in SAP AI Core

Create a Destination in SAP BTP Cockpit with a name such as GENERATIVE_AI_HUB and the following info:

 

# Destination
Name: URL: <AI_API_URL>/v2 # Eg. https://api.ai....ml.hana.ondemand.com/v2 <- Note the suffix /v2
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID: <CLIENT_ID>
Client Secret: <CLIENT_SECRET>
Token Service Url: <TOKEN_URL>/oauth2/token

## Additional Properties
HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group: default
URL.headers.Content-Type: application/json

 

Please note that the URL is suffixed with /v2 in order to align with cap-llm-plugin usage.

3. Prepare for Deployment

To generate the necessary files needed for deployment such as xs-app.json and mta.yaml, execute the below command.

 

npm run deploy-config

 

4. Add a route to Destination for LLM API

Now, add an API route to proxy API calls for LLM via the Destination created in the xs-app.json file.

 

{
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/api/(.*)$",
      "target": "/inference/deployments/<DEPLOYMENT_ID>/$1",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "destination": "GENERATIVE_AI_HUB",
      "csrfProtection": true
    },
    ...
  ]
}

 

5. Call the LLM API

Now, you can call the LLM API from your code. To demonstrate, I'm using the modern Browser fetch to make a REST API call.

 

// first get relative module path
const sModulePrefix = this.getOwnerComponent().getManifestEntry("/sap.app/id");

// make an API call to an LLM
const res = await fetch(
  `${sModulePrefix}/api/chat/completions?api-version=2024-02-01`,
  {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "X-CSRF-Token": "<X-CSRF-Token>", // recommended for security. You can disable this for testing by setting `csrfProtection: false` in `xs-app.json` for `/api` route
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "AI-Resource-Group": "default", // mandatory
    },
    credentials: "same-origin",
    body: JSON.stringify({
      messages: [
        {
          role: "system",
          content: "Write a TL;DR/summary of the user content in a paragraph.",
        },
        {
          role: "user",
          content: sTxtInput,
        },
      ],
    }),
  }
)

 

6. Deploy the SAPUI5 app

Now, you can build the MTA app and deploy it by executing the following commands:

 

# Build an MTAR archive
mbt build

# Deploy the MTAR archive to logged in space
npm run deploy

 

6. Bonus: How to configure during development

The above steps outlined the steps to deploy an SAPUI5 integrated with LLM capabilities. Every time you make a change, the app needs to get built and deployed again which is not a good developer experience. Here in the subsequent steps, you can follow to connect your dev/local setup with BTP Destination and XSUAA services to work seamlessly during development whether in SAP Business Application Studio or in VS Code.

6a. Deploy the SAPUI5 app once

You must deploy your SAPUI5 app at least once, if not done already to consume Destinations with OAuth2ClientCredentials work locally. This setup requires the credentials to BTP Destinations and XSUAA services locally to work properly.

 

# Build an MTAR archive
mbt build

# Deploy the MTAR archive to logged in space
npm run deploy

 

6b. Create a default-env.json

Now, create a default-env.json file with the following content and replace the placeholders with actual credentials.

 

{
  "VCAP_SERVICES": {
    "destination": [
      {
        "credentials": {
          "clientid": "<CLIENT_ID>",
          "clientsecret": "<CLIENT_SECRET>",
          "url": "<AUTH_URL>",
          "uri": "<DESTINATION_URI>"
        },
        "label": "destination",
        "name": "<INSTANCE_NAME>",
        "tags": ["destination", "conn", "connsvc"]
      }
    ],
    "xsuaa": [
      {
        "credentials": {
          "clientid": "<CLIENT_ID>",
          "clientsecret": "<CLIENT_SECRET>",
          "url": "<AUTH_URL>"
        },
        "label": "xsuaa",
        "tags": ["xsuaa"]
      }
    ]
  }
}

 

6c. Install UI5 Middleware Plugin for Approuter

Now, install the ui5-middleware-approuter plugin, which wraps the actual @sap/approuter package to make the destination and routing work locally.

 

npm install -D ui5-middleware-approuter

 

6d. Update UI5 configuration to use the middleware

Now, update the ui5.yaml and ui5-local.yaml to add ui5-middleware-approuter as a custom middleware

 

...
server:
  customerMiddleware:
    ...
    - name: ui5-middleware-approuter
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        authenticationMethod: "route"
        xsappJson: "./xs-app.json"
        appendAuthRoute: true # trigger auth, inorder to connect to destination service

 

Now, start running your UI5 app server locally and calls to /api endpoint now would get proxied over to LLMs running in SAP AI Core 😀.

 

npm start

 

This concludes the step-by-step approach to integrating LLMs with your SAPUI5 apps. In the subsequent blogs, I'll walk you through practical use-case implementations - A text Summarizer and an AI Chatbot Assistant in SAP Freestyle UI. Please note, that the techniques mentioned above work for SAP Fiori Elements apps too.

If you have specific questions on the approach or other interesting Generative AI topics to know, please feel free to comment below. I'm all ears! 👂

entry.title: Preparing for Google’s Third-Party Cookie Deprecation
entry.content:
Browser Are Ending Third-Party Cookies Support

Browser manufacturers are ending support of third-party cookies to prevent usage tracking for data privacy reasons. Google Chrome is the most prominent example, with its third-party cookie deprecation program. This might impact your SAP solutions, especially regarding session management and single sign-on authentication.

As of Q1 2024, Google is restricting third-party cookies for 1% of Chrome users and plans on ramping up to 100% starting Q1 2025. As a consequence, end users might already be experiencing issues with their web applications running in Google Chrome, such as broken authentication flows or content sections missing or not behaving properly.

For a brief introduction to the subject, check out Google’s dedicated documentation.

If your solution is intended to run in an embedded iFrame or relies on custom domains other than ondemand.com or sapcloud.cn, it is most probably affected by this change.

While we, SAP, are doing our best to keep track of Google’s third-party cookie deprecation project to provide information and possible solutions, we recommend following Google’s official documentation on the subject for the most up to date timelines, guidelines, and recommended solutions.

Temporary Mitigation Strategy

We, SAP, have made sure to secure exceptions for the most common central entry points hosted on SAP-owned domains: ondemand.com, cloud.sap and sapcloud.cn. Your application can continue to be integrated into those entry points without any need for modification until the exceptions expire on December 27th, 2024.

Planning Ahead

If your solution is affected, we strongly recommend testing your solution to identify any potential breakage. Here are resources we strongly recommend reading:

  • Google’s official guidelines for testing.
  • Our testing guide tailored to SAP solutions.
  • Requesting a deprecation trial token.
  • Getting and applying a deprecation trial token.
  • Our solution guide to implement a permanent solution.

Please note that the deprecation trial token is only a temporary fix to keep your solution running with third-party cookies until the end of the grace period, and beyond if you have deployed the token. You should have a final mitigation strategy and solution in place by the end of December 2024.

Google's Official Solutions

Google has come up with different solutions to mitigate the third-party cookie deprecation. While we are still investigating these solutions to make them especially suited for SAP solutions, we encourage you to start investigating in a mitigation strategy for your SAP solution as soon as possible.

  • Migrate your solution to a common super domain.
  • Storage Access API.
  • Cookies Having Independent Partitioned State (CHIPS).

Please watch this space for further updates as we make progress on the topic.

Read More on the Subject

To go further, make sure to read the existing blogs and documentation below tailored to specific SAP solution:

  • SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP / SAP Web Dispatcher:
    • Required for SAP GUI for HTML, Web Dynpro ABAP, Floorplan Manager & Business Server Pages (BSP): 3406925 - Injecting the "Partitioned" Cookie Attribute using ICM / SAP Web Dispatcher Rewrite Rules
    • SAP GUI for HTML (additional): 3414586 Webgui cookies: set own cookies to partitioned
  • SAP NetWeaver Application Server for Java / SAP Web Dispatcher:
    • Required for Web Dynpro JAVA:
      3406925 - Injecting the "Partitioned" Cookie Attribute using ICM / SAP Web Dispatcher Rewrite Rules
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
    • SAP Note 3412305 - How does 3rd Party Cookies being deprecated in Chrome affect SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
    • Blog Post "Product Alert Update Dec 19 – SAP Analytics Cloud and Google Chrome"
    • Blog Post "Third-Party Cookies and SAP Analytics Cloud"
  • SAP Enable Now and Companion - SAP Note 3321284 - SAP Companion authentication issue in Chrome or Edge - SAP for Me.
  • SAP SuccessFactors - Blog Post "SAP SuccessFactors Third Party Cookies and Common Super Domain Rollout"
  • SAP BTP – Blog Post "SAP BTP and Third-Party Cookies Deprecation"
  • SAP BTP, Neo Environment - Blog Post on the Custom Domain service in SAP BTP (Neo)
  • SAP BUILD Work Zone - Blog Post "Are You Affected by the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation?"
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Blog Post "Handling Third Party Cookies Deprecation in Identity Authentication"
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services: Identity Authentication – "Third-Party Cookies and SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service" 

If you are specifically interested in the potential impact on SAP BTP, we. Our first installment of the blog series is a great starting point to get more familiar with the topic, and find out whether your application is affected by this change. If you haven’t already, we recommend reading through it to know whether your solution is affected by the third-party cookie deprecation.

entry.title: GRC Process Control: How CCM can be leveraged to monitor HANA Databases
entry.content:
HANA databases often serve as repositories for organization's critical financial data, making them subject to stringent regulatory requirements such as SOX. Leveraging SAP GRC Process Control Continuous Controls Monitoring (CCM) module provides an effective solution to record and monitor database activities in real-time. This article comprehensively covers the steps to setup an automated control in GRC PC for HANA database. I am taking the below real time use case and creating an automated ITGC control in PC to monitor HANA Database and report the exceptions.

 

Use case:

The HANA Database administrators often make configuration changes in HANA DB such as parameters related to memory allocation, disk storage, network settings, security configuration, etc.  Such changes are generally allowed to be happened using SYSTEM account. In this case, there's a need to have a control to review who actually used the SYSTEM account and made the changes to meet the SOX compliance objectives. GRC PC can be leveraged to automate the control monitoring and reporting by following below steps.

 

I am using the standard view "SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENT_HISTORY" that stores the database global parameters (refer below screenshot). The field USER_NAME shows the actual database user id who made the change and APPLICATION_USER_NAME is the one who used the DB user (USER_NAME) to make the change and APPLICATION_NAME shows the application where the change is initiated from like HDBStudio, HANa Cockpit or from OS level etc.

 

Let's say only the SYSTEM user is allowed to make configuration changes. The control requirement is to review the logs at the end of each month and report any changes made by the users other than SYSTEM.

 

 

 

Let's define a control in PC and automate monitoring. To achieve it, perform below steps.

  1. Create a calculation view in HANA database to list the configuration changes and send it to process control.
  2. Establish a connection between PC and HANA Database and assign the connector to Integration scenario "AM"
  3. Create a Data source in PC
  4. Create a Business Rule in PC
  5. Create an automated Control in PC and assign the owner (agent)
  6. Assign business rule to the control.
  7. Schedule an automated monitoring job

 

Pre-requisites:

  • GRC system - At least on 10.1 (Please refer to SAP Product Availability Matrix for more information)
  • GRC Plugin to be installed on HANA Database - Delivery Unit Deployment (Ref: 2589878 - Installing or upgrading HANA GRC Plug-in - SAP for Me)

 

  • Create a calculation view in HANA database:

GRC process control can read only the calculation view result returned from the Hana data base. So, you need to create a calculation view in Hana database by including the columns from the view to be returned. (Best practice is to minimize the amount of data to be passed to PC by applying conditions and calculations at HANA database side rather than passing large chunk of data to PC and apply the filters and calculations there).

Limitations:

  1. PC supports only "SQL Script" type calculation view.
  2. PC supports only four datatypes - DATE, INTEGER, NVARCHAR and DECIMAL. If any fields of other datatype, their type to be converted to one of the supported data type.
  3. Calculation view must have at least a measure field.

Here's a sample script for calculation view.

 Note: Customers who have Enterprise HANA is in place (do not allow development on HANA tenant databases) can achieve this by creating virtual tables. (I am going to cover this with another use case very soon)

  • Establish a connection between PC and HANA Database:
    • Create HANA Database connector in GRC system using the transaction code DBCO
    • Create a connector using SM59 (type: "L")
    • Define Connector and Connector group in GRC and assign the connector to connector group.
    • Maintain Connection settings - assign the Hana DB connector to the integration scenario (AM - automated monitoring")

The remaining steps are common for all automated controls. I am going to cover those steps in a separate blog.

Hope you enjoyed reading this blog. In case of any queries, let me know in comment or get in touch with me.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

entry.title: Innovating Towards a Sustainable Future: Insights from Rolf Adam
entry.content:
Staying Grounded in SAP Excellence

Reflecting on the industry's deep-rooted reliance on SAP, Rolf Adam underscores the steadfast commitment of major oil and gas companies to SAP solutions. With a legacy of nearly four decades, SAP remains the backbone for optimizing operations and navigating the industry's intricate processes efficiently. As the sector transitions towards renewable energy sources like Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF), SAP's "Clean Core" strategy and future-forward solutions, such as S/4HANA, offer a pathway for driving sustainable innovation and operational resilience.

Partnering for Success: A Collaborative Approach

Implico's alignment with SAP's product roadmap and commitment to technological innovation exemplify the successful partnership dynamics crucial for supporting industry needs. By leveraging SAP's market-leading solutions like SAP SDM and RFNO, Implico sets industry benchmarks in downstream business operations, equipping customers with cutting-edge tools and insights for enhanced performance.

Embracing Cloud Technologies: A Strategic Imperative

Amidst the industry's evolving landscape, the discussion around cloud adoption and licenses surfaces as a critical consideration. As industry dynamics necessitate the adoption of cloud-based solutions, Implico's strategic approach to cloud integration underscores a commitment to agile and scalable business models, paving the way for streamlined processes and enhanced customer experiences.

Bridging Innovation with Business Value: The Role of SAP BTP

SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP) emerges as a catalyst for future-proofing solutions and unlocking new realms of innovation. Positioned as a playground for customization and add-ons, BTP empowers companies like Implico to extend their offerings and deliver tailor-made solutions that align with industry demands. By embracing AI technologies and leveraging the scalability of BTP, Implico charts a course towards continuous innovation and customer-centric excellence.

Charting the Future Course: A Confluence of Expertise and Vision

As the industry strides towards a future marked by sustainability, digitalization, and transformative growth, the insights shared by Rolf Adam offer a compelling narrative on SAP's pivotal role in shaping the oil, gas, and energy landscape. By staying rooted in industry expertise, fostering collaborative partnerships, and embracing cutting-edge technologies like BTP, companies like Implico exemplify a commitment to driving industry-wide progress and innovation.

Conclusion

The interview with Rolf Adam serves as a testament to the industry's resilience, adaptability, and unwavering commitment to excellence amidst transformative industry shifts. As SAP solutions continue to evolve, empowering businesses to embrace sustainability, digitalization, and efficiency, the oil, gas, and energy sector stands poised at the cusp of a new era of innovation, growth, and sustainable progress.

 

Find the full interview here: https://www.implico.com/news_posts/e3-magazin/

 

SAP S/4HANA SDM: This tool offers a cohesive approach to the trade and transport of liquids and half-solids, integrating seamlessly with the SAP system to streamline processes from demand forecasting to invoicing, thereby facilitating smoother operations in hydrocarbon product trading.

SAP S/4HANA RFNO: A dedicated solution for managing service station networks, it caters to various service station types, offering functionalities like wet goods management and loyalty card management, aiming to enhance the fuel retail experience.

Implico’s Quality Assurance Program for SAP solutions: Implico wants to ensure the implementation of SAP SDM/RFNO solutions is professional and error-free – not only when doing so ourselves but also when external companies are contracted to oversee implementation. This is why we also offer two partner services that are designed to supplement the implementation projects based on a methodological approach and with highly skilled consultants, both of which help to achieve the defined targets: the Partner Governance Service and the Partner Advisor Service.

entry.title: Re: DTP step stuck at Extract. from Datastore step in Dat...
entry.content:
Hello @srini_walgreens 

unfortunately I don't understand every detail you describe ... e.g. what about the activation processes during data extraction? For me, your description should be a bit more precise.

Questions: Why do you use 25 processes in parallel for extraction? Do you have a package size of 50.000 records or higher? How many records are transferred in these 8 different delta extractions during the day?

I would try to reduce number of parallel extraction processes to 5 max, but increase the package size to 250.000 ... depending of your width of one record (no of fields).

I would not have any other idea.

BR, Martin
